content.full_text:
DANIEL E. LUNGREN
Attorney General
State of Caldornia
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE
1515 K STREET. SUITE 511
P.O. BOX 944255
SACRAMENTO 94244-2550
(916) 324-s166
May 8, 1991
John l-arson, Chairman
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Mr. Larson:
Opinion No. 91-504
We have received a request from Assemblyman Bill Jones, for an opinion of the
Attorney General on the following question:
What restrictions exist on the use of funds collected by a committee primarily
formed to support or oppose a statewide ballot measure after the election?
It is the policy of our office to solicit the views of all interested parties prior to
issuing an opinion. Yonr comments regarding the question presented would be
appreciated. If possible, a response by June 6, 7997, would be most helpful; materials
received after such date will nonetheless be considered. Views submitted will be
treated by our office as public records under the Public Records Act. Please address
your views to: Deputy Attorney General Rodney O. Ulyquist, 110 West A Street, Suite
700, San Diego, CA 92101; telephone (619) 237-7182.
Sincerely,
DANIEL E. LUNGREN
SACRAMENTO 94244-2550
NK:lac
Chief, Opinion

sections.question: What restrictions exist on the use of funds collected by a committee primarily formed to support or oppose a statewide ballot measure after the election?
sections.question_synthetic: The Attorney General seeks FPPC input on what restrictions apply to post-election use of funds by committees formed to support or oppose statewide ballot measures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request to FPPC from the Attorney General's office, not an FPPC advice letter. No formal CONCLUSION section present. The document requests FPPC input rather than providing FPPC guidance. Minor OCR errors noted (e.g., 'Caldornia' for 'California', '1997' appears to be '1991' context error).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: What restrictions exist on the use of funds collected by a committee primarily formed to support or oppose a statewide ballot measure after the election?

embedding.summary: Attorney General Daniel Lungren requests the California FPPC's views on restrictions governing post-election use of funds by ballot measure committees, as part of the Attorney General's process for issuing Opinion No. 91-504 on behalf of Assemblyman Bill Jones.
